description: All authored Freddy Fractions dialogue. Keys map 1:1 to MP3 filenames in /public/audio/. Lines may contain {{NAME}} which is split at build time so the kid's name MP3 is stitched at runtime (see PRD §3.11). Most lines use 'kid' as a generic address term — Freddy calls everyone 'kid'. The {{NAME}} slot is reserved for the single recognition beat right after the kid types their name (`onboarding_response`); everywhere else, 'kid' keeps the rhythm and avoids name-stitch breaks.

voice.notes: Super Mario meets Jersey Shore — Italian-American chef energy, warm, lovable, expressive. Selected from ElevenLabs Voice Library by Jason. Voice must be added to the account's voice library before the API can call it (Voice Library → Add to my voices).

lines.onboarding_greeting: Heyyy, welcome to SuperSlice! I'm Freddy Fractions. What's your name, kid?
lines.onboarding_response: {{NAME}}, beautiful name. Alright, lemme show ya how this works.
lines.explore_intro_2: Slicer and glove are right down there.
lines.explore_intro_4: Deliveries go in the box. Have a play around.
lines.explore_cue: Just tap me on the shoulder when you're done messin' around.
lines.aha_setup: Hey kid, want to see something cool? I just pulled this fresh pizza outta the oven — it's already cut in half. Try slicing one of those halves once more, just for me.
lines.aha_wrong_slice: Hey, nice cut! But try slicing one of the halves we already made, alright?
lines.aha_stuck: C'mon, kid — try tapping the slicer on one of those halves.
lines.aha_compare_prompt: Beautiful! Now drag those two quarters right next to a half. Tell me what you notice.
lines.aha_not_equal: Hmm, those ain't quite the same yet. Try moving them right up against each other.
lines.aha_stuck_compare: Slide the quarters right up against the half, kid. You'll see it.
lines.aha_reveal: Whoa, kid! Look at that — one half is the SAME as two quarters! You just made fraction equivalence! Bellissimo!
lines.lesson_win: Bellissimo, kid! You put it back together — just like a real pizza chef!
lines.lesson_intro: Alright kid, two hungry friends just ordered. One pizza, gotta split it fair. Can you slice it right down the middle so each friend gets the SAME amount?
lines.lesson_react_halves: Boom — two equal halves! Each friend gets one half. Now, two MORE friends showed up. Four hungry kids, same pizza. Can you cut each half one more time?
lines.lesson_stuck_halves: Try the slicer on the pizza, kid. Cut it right down the middle.
lines.lesson_react_quarters: Four equal quarters! Each friend gets one piece. But here's where it gets cool...
lines.lesson_wrong_eighths: Hey, those pieces are getting tiny! Try slicing one of those bigger halves instead.
lines.lesson_stuck_quarters: Tap the slicer on one of those halves, kid. Cut it in half again.
lines.lesson_compare_prompt: Now drag two of those quarters right up next to one half. Tell me what you notice.
lines.lesson_not_equal: Get them right up against each other, kid. Real close.
lines.lesson_stuck_compare: Grab TWO quarter pieces and slide them right next to that half. You'll see it.
lines.lesson_reveal: Whoa, {{NAME}}! Look at that — one half is the EXACT same size as two quarters. That's fraction equivalence. One-half equals two-quarters! Bellissimo!
lines.lesson_end: You did it, kid! You just proved that one-half equals two-quarters. That's real math. Bellissimo!
lines.react_eighths: Eighths! Look at all those slices.
